checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet=3525595000 (FY2015) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=3572790000 (FY2016), only a 1.3% increase
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent reporting stops at value=443539000 (2019-12-31); no AR balance data provided for 2020-2025 in this dataset
evidence.location: 10-K balance sheet (2019-12-31), last AR data point in series

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=2750449000 (FY2016) grew to RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=3946390000 (FY2024), only ~43.5% over the same period
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=11844000 (2016-12-31) rose to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=304607000 (2023-12-31), a >25x increase driven by acquisitions

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=167528000 (FY2023) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=422158000 (FY2023) — operating cash flow substantially exceeds net income, consistent with conservative/high-quality earnings rather than aggressive accrual-based income recognition

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or similar inventory concept is present anywhere in the provided financial_series_point_in_time data, consistent with Hub Group's asset-light logistics/freight brokerage business model where inventory is immaterial
evidence.location: Absence of inventory line item across all filings in dataset

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=889505000 on RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=5340490000 (FY2022) vs GrossProfit=521070000 on RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=3668117000 (FY2019) — margin expansion tracks the 2021-2022 freight market boom rather than appearing anomalous

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=73559000 (FY2020) rose to NetIncomeLoss=171474000 (FY2021) (+133%), then NetIncomeLoss=356948000 (FY2022) (+108%), then fell to NetIncomeLoss=167528000 (FY2023) (-53%) and NetIncomeLoss=103993000 (FY2024) (-38%) — large swings track the freight cycle rather than being smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=8300000 (2020-12-31) increased to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=20100000 (2021-12-31) and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=38600000 (2022-12-31), rising faster than revenue growth over the same period, indicating reserve build-up rather than reserve erosion

AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent declined to 34700000 (2023-12-31) and 26700000 (2024-12-31) in tandem with revenue declines (RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=4202585000 FY2023 and 3946390000 FY2024 vs 5340490000 FY2022), a proportionate rather than anomalous decline

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2018-01-05 (accession 0001564590-18-000122) restating FY2015 financial statements, together with 10-Q/A filings on the same date for periods 2016-09-30 (0001564590-18-026523... reissued), 2017-03-31, 2017-06-30, and 2017-09-30 (accessions 0001564590-18-000123, 0001564590-18-000124, 0001564590-18-000126), plus 8-K and 8-K/A filings dated 2018-01-05
UPLOAD filing_date=2017-12-21 and CORRESP filing_date=2018-01-17 / UPLOAD filing_date=2018-01-18 indicate SEC staff correspondence immediately surrounding the amended filings

Cluster of UPLOAD/CORRESP filings in 2022 (2022-07-06, 2022-07-20, 2022-08-02, 2022-08-16, 2022-08-19, 2022-09-07, 2022-09-21, 2022-10-07) suggests an extended SEC comment-letter review process, though no subsequent restatement followed

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly XBRL concepts from fiscal 2007/2008 through Q3 2025 across Assets, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent (through 2019), Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow, and a full filing chronology from 2007-2026, providing reasonably long coverage, though AccountsReceivableNetCurrent and InventoryNet data are absent for 2020-2025, limiting recency of receivables analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reclassification/restatement of previously issued financial statements: Hub Group filed a 10-K/A for FY2015 and 10-Q/A for Q3 2016 and Q1-Q3 2017 simultaneously on 2018-01-05, alongside 8-K/8-K/A filings, most likely reflecting a correction to gross-vs-net (principal vs. agent) presentation of purchased transportation/freight costs against revenue, which restates Revenue, Cost of Services, and Gross Profit line items across multiple prior periods without necessarily changing bottom-line Net Income.

rationale_evidence.quote: CostOfServices annual value=3112900000 (FY2015) reported in 10-K filed 2018-02-28, differing presentation basis from earlier CostOfServices figures reported prior to the amendment cycle

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Aggressive roll-up acquisition strategy (e.g., Estenson Logistics 2018, TAGG/other logistics acquisitions) has driven Goodwill from Goodwill=262376000 (2016-12-31) to Goodwill=814309000 (2024-12-31), a ~210% increase, far outpacing the ~43% growth in revenue (RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=2750449000 FY2016 to 3946390000 FY2024). This creates elevated impairment risk and potential overstatement of carrying values if acquired businesses underperform, particularly as revenue declined in FY2023-FY2024 while goodwill kept rising.

rationale_evidence.quote: RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax=5340490000 (FY2022) declined to 4202585000 (FY2023) and 3946390000 (FY2024) while Goodwill continued to rise from 629402000 (2022-12-31) to 733695000 (2023-12-31) to 814309000 (2024-12-31)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables grew substantially faster than revenue in at least one identifiable period (2015→2016), and the allowance for doubtful accounts subsequently required a >4x build-up (from AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=8300000 in 2020 to 38600000 in 2022), suggesting either under-reserving in earlier periods or rapid credit-quality deterioration during the acquisition-driven growth phase, potentially masking softer receivable quality; however, lack of AR balance data after 2019 limits confirmation of the most recent trend.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=379987000 (2015-12-31) to 473608000 (2016-12-31), +24.6%, vs SalesRevenueServicesNet +1.3% over the same period

top_signals: Confirmed restatement event: 10-K/A (FY2015) plus 10-Q/A for Q3 2016 and Q1-Q3 2017 all filed 2018-01-05 alongside 8-K/8-K/A, preceded by SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP correspondence in Dec 2017-Jan 2018, indicating a formal correction of previously issued financial statements
Goodwill grew ~210% (262M in 2016 to 814M in 2024) while revenue grew only ~43% over the same span, reflecting an aggressive M&A roll-up strategy with attendant impairment risk

Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in at least one period (2015→2016: +24.6% vs +1.3%), and allowance for doubtful accounts subsequently quadrupled from 2020-2022, though post-2019 AR balances are missing from the dataset limiting full verification

Extended secondary SEC comment-letter correspondence cluster in mid-to-late 2022 (multiple UPLOAD/CORRESP filings) suggests renewed regulatory scrutiny, though it did not result in a subsequent restatement

Operating cash flow has consistently and substantially exceeded net income (e.g., FY2023 OCF=422M vs NI=168M), which is a mitigating factor suggesting underlying earnings quality is not artificially inflated via accruals
